step_by_step_thinking: Increased size of filler particles would likely affect the rheological property known as viscosity. Larger particles require more force to move within the matrix, increasing internal friction and thus decreasing flow ability. Therefore, larger particles will decrease the fluid-like behavior typical of lower-viscosity liquids, aligning with option D.
raw_response:
Increased size of filler particles would likely affect the rheological property known as viscosity. Larger particles require more force to move within the matrix, increasing internal friction and thus decreasing flow ability. Therefore, larger particles will decrease the fluid-like behavior typical of lower-viscosity liquids, aligning with option D.
\boxed{D}
